method_atom: A duality that maps a strongly-interacting many-body system at finite temperature onto an equivalent higher-dimensional geometric description possessing a horizon, allowing collective long-wavelength behavior to be computed geometrically. The long-wavelength, low-frequency limit of two-point response (correlation) functions yields the system's dissipative transport coefficients, and the slowly-relaxing collective hydrodynamic modes are placed in one-to-one correspondence with the lowest-lying damped relaxational excitations of the dual geometric background. Within this class of dual descriptions one can prove a universal lower bound on the ratio of a dissipative transport coefficient to the entropy density.
object_atom: The shear viscosity, entropy, and hydrodynamic behavior of strongly-coupled finite-temperature quantum field theories that admit gravity duals, mapped to the quasinormal modes of an anti-de Sitter black p-brane spacetime, with implications for real thermal systems such as the quark-gluon plasma.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the strong-coupling/geometry duality plus the extraction of transport coefficients and relaxational modes from low-frequency response functions and a universal transport-to-entropy bound; the domain-specific object is the thermal quantum field theory's viscosity and its dual AdS black-brane geometry.
